message.content:
THIS IS A READ-ONLY AUDIT. Do NOT edit any code, config, or report. Do NOT modify the verifier. Repo: /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent (branch gate-inversion @ b67506a). Champion = bot/outline-agent-box @ df4118a.

FRAMING: The operator suspects the "tail gate" — everything AFTER section-compose that can DROP / REWRITE / REMOVE content on FAITHFULNESS grounds — is destroying high-value SYNTHESIS, INSIGHT, and ANALYSIS, which are the biggest RACE dimensions (Insight 0.32, Comprehensiveness 0.29, Instruction-Following 0.25, Readability 0.14). The classic tension: cross-source synthesis / interpretation / implications may lack a single-source span citation and thus fail per-sentence NLI grounding -> get DROPPED -> the report loses exactly the analysis that scores highest. That is the "faithfulness ghost."

CONSTRAINT: faithfulness is FROZEN — this audit must NOT propose editing provenance_generator.py / strict_verify / NLI / the drop rule / D8 thresholds. Its job is to (a) CHARACTERIZE and QUANTIFY what the tail drops and whether high-value insight is among it, and (b) find faithfulness-SAFE levers (upstream grounding, D8 rescue, ADJUSTABLE non-frozen layers, section routing) that preserve insight WITHOUT touching the verifier. BUT be brutally honest: if the FROZEN verifier itself is the culprit, SAY SO plainly and quantify the cost — the operator needs the truth to decide, even though we won't touch it without their explicit call.

MAP the entire tail gate. Enumerate EVERY stage from the end of section-compose through the final assembled report.md that can DROP, REMOVE, REWRITE, or SUPPRESS content. For EACH stage give: file:function:line; exactly WHAT it removes; the precise CRITERION (e.g. per-sentence NLI entailment threshold, span-grounding requirement, key-findings refilter, chrome/render-seam removal, D8 adjudication drop, drop_disclosure); whether it operates per-SENTENCE or per-block; and CLASSIFY it FROZEN (provenance_generator.py / strict_verify / NLI / D8 thresholds / drop rule) vs ADJUSTABLE (non-frozen render/compose/key_findings/depth layers). CRITICALLY, for each stage judge: can it drop SYNTHESIS / INTERPRETATION / CROSS-SOURCE / ANALYSIS sentences (multi-source or inferential claims) as opposed to only atomic single-source facts? Trace the strict_verify drop condition precisely — does a sentence need a single supporting span, and how are multi-source synthesis sentences handled (dropped? split? attributed to strongest source?). Also: does the D8 four-role adjudication ADD drops or RESCUE claims? Return a structured stage-by-stage table.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
"""Strict verifier per CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3.

Per-sentence check enforcing:
  (a) at least one provenance token present
  (b) every token references an evidence_id in the pool
  (c) span bounds are valid (0 <= start <= end <= len(text))
  (d) every decimal in the sentence appears in the cited spans
  (e) sentence and span share >= MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP content words
  (f) I-bug-092: span semantically ENTAILS the sentence's specific claims
      (gated behind PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, defaults off)

Checks (a)-(e) are mechanical "provenance hygiene" — they bound a claim to
the same TOPIC as its span. Check (f) is "provenance correctness" — it
verifies the span actually entails the sentence's specific facts. The
audit on 2026-05-09 found that a capable generator can pass (a)-(e) by
producing topically-overlapping prose that introduces unsourced specifics
(e.g. inserting "pancreatic β-cells", "lipid metabolism", "energy storage"
into a span that says only "synergistic actions on insulin secretion,
glucagon suppression, appetite regulation, and adipocyte metabolism").

Returns a (verifier_pass: bool, drop_reason: str | None) pair, or directly
constructs a VerifiedSentence for use by the generator orchestrator.

Tunables (read at call time so tests can override):
  PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP — minimum shared content words (default 2)
  PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT       — "off" | "warn" | "enforce" (default)
                                       off:     skip check (f), pre-I-bug-092 behavior
                                                — operator override; production runs
                                                  ship without provenance-correctness
                                                  enforcement
                                       warn:    run check (f), log violations,
                                                do NOT drop (collect telemetry)
                                       enforce: run check (f), drop on
                                                NEUTRAL or CONTRADICTED verdict
                                                (default per I-bug-095, validated
                                                empirically by I-bug-094 live test)
  PG_ENTAILMENT_MODEL               — entailment judge model
                                       (default: google/gemma-4-31b-it,
                                        the two-family evaluator)
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import functools
import json
import logging
import os
import re

from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.provenance import (
    ProvenanceToken,
    extract_tokens,
    get_span_text,
    strip_tokens,
    validate_token_against_pool,
)
# I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): CJK / multilingual-aware content tokenization + the
# unsegmentable-script fail-closed guard for the strict_verify overlap floor.
from src.polaris_graph.generator.script_aware_grounding import (
    extra_script_tokens,
    has_unsegmentable_content,
)
from src.polaris_graph.clinical_generator.verified_report import (
    DropReason,
    VerifiedSentence,
)
from src.polaris_graph.clinical_retrieval.evidence_pool import EvidencePool

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Tunables
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

DEFAULT_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP = 2

# Minimal English stopword list. Strict-verify's content-word overlap should
# count substantive vocabulary, not function words. This is intentionally
# small — the rule is conservative ("at least 2 shared content words")

386	def verify_sentence(
387	    sentence_text: str,
388	    pool: EvidencePool,
389	    min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
390	    is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
391	) -> tuple[bool, DropReason | None]:
392	    """Return (verifier_pass, drop_reason) for `sentence_text` against `pool`.
393	
394	    Pass: returns (True, None).
395	    Fail: returns (False, drop_reason) with reason from DropReason literal.
396	
397	    Implements CLAUDE.md §9.1 invariant 3 in order:
398	      1. at least one well-formed token        → no_provenance_token
399	      2. every token references known source_id → invalid_token
400	      3. spans within source bounds            → span_out_of_range
401	      4. every decimal in sentence in spans    → numeric_mismatch
402	      5. >=N shared content words              → overlap_too_low
403	      6. (I-bug-092) span entails sentence     → entailment_failed
404	         (gated by PG_STRICT_VERIFY_ENTAILMENT, off by default)
405	
406	    If `is_synthesis_claim=True` AND the sentence has no tokens, return
407	    (True, None) — synthesis claims pass without provenance by definition
408	    (I-f5-006). If a synthesis claim DOES carry tokens, that's a generator
409	    bug; we still run token checks so the underlying invariant holds.
410	    """
411	    threshold = (
412	        min_content_overlap
413	        if min_content_overlap is not None
414	        else _min_overlap_threshold()
415	    )
416	
417	    tokens = extract_tokens(sentence_text)
418	    if not tokens:
419	        if is_synthesis_claim:
420	            return True, None
421	        return False, "no_provenance_token"
422	
423	    # Validate each token against the pool. Collect span texts for
424	    # later numeric + overlap checks.
425	    span_texts: list[str] = []
426	    for token in tokens:
427	        reason = validate_token_against_pool(token, pool)
428	        if reason == "invalid_token":
429	            return False, "invalid_token"
430	        if reason == "span_out_of_range":
431	            return False, "span_out_of_range"
432	        # Token valid — fetch its span text
433	        span = get_span_text(token, pool)
434	        if span is None:
435	            # Belt-and-suspenders: token validated but get_span_text said no.
436	            return False, "span_out_of_range"
437	        span_texts.append(span)
438	
439	    sentence_clean = strip_tokens(sentence_text)
440	    combined_span = " ".join(span_texts)
441	
442	    # Decimal match: every decimal in sentence must appear in the combined
443	    # span text. Permits the spans to contain MORE decimals than the
444	    # sentence cites — that's fine; the constraint is one-way.
445	    sentence_decimals = _decimals(sentence_clean)
446	    span_decimals = _decimals(combined_span)
447	    if not sentence_decimals.issubset(span_decimals):
448	        return False, "numeric_mismatch"
449	
450	    # I-deepfix-001 (Wave-2) PERCENT-ROLE re-check (parity with
451	    # provenance_generator.verify_sentence_provenance). A printed percent
452	    # ("15%", "15 percent") is a PERCENT claim: it must be carried by a cited
453	    # span AS A PERCENT, not merely as a bare digit that coincidentally equals
454	    # the value (a page number / year / count). The decimal check above passes a
455	    # bare "15" via the number union; this compares PERCENT-vs-PERCENT
456	    # (`_percents`, same regex both sides). Span percents are read PER cited span
457	    # text (not the joined string) so a "15" ending one span and a "%" starting
458	    # the next can never be glued into a spurious percent. STRICTLY ADDITIVE:
459	    # only ADDS a drop; nothing grounded by a genuine in-span percent is newly
460	    # dropped. Default-ON; PG_PROVENANCE_PERCENT_ROLE_MATCH=0 reverts byte-identical.
461	    if _percent_role_match_enabled():
462	        sentence_percents = _percents(sentence_clean)
463	        if sentence_percents:
464	            span_percents: set[str] = set()
465	            for _span in span_texts:
466	                span_percents |= _percents(_span)
467	            if not sentence_percents.issubset(span_percents):
468	                return False, "percent_not_in_cited_span"
469	
470	    # I-deepfix-001 (P5) EPISTEMIC-QUALIFIER RETENTION re-check. The composer can
471	    # copy a numeral's VALUE but drop the hedge / scope qualifier the cited span
472	    # binds to it ("some estimates suggest 46% ... under a complementary-software
473	    # scenario" -> a flat "46%"). For each cited span, if a SUBSTANTIVE numeral in
474	    # the span (decimal / percent — never a bare count / year) also appears in the
475	    # sentence AND an epistemic marker sits within
476	    # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_PROXIMITY_TOKENS of THAT numeral IN THE SPAN, the
477	    # sentence must itself carry a marker; else it dropped a binding qualifier ->
478	    # (False, "binding_qualifier_dropped"). Span percents/decimals are read PER
479	    # cited span (not the joined string) so a marker in one span can never bind a
480	    # numeral in another. STRICTLY ADDITIVE: only ADDS a drop; the verbatim K-span
481	    # fallback retains the qualifier by construction, so a genuine hedged finding
482	    # still ships. Default-ON; PG_STRICT_VERIFY_QUALIFIER_RETENTION=0 reverts
483	    # byte-identical. Read at call time.
484	    if _qualifier_retention_enabled():
485	        marker_re = _qualifier_marker_re(_qualifier_lexicon())
486	        if not _has_epistemic_marker(sentence_clean, marker_re):
487	            window = _qualifier_proximity_tokens()
488	            sentence_numbers = _decimals(sentence_clean)
489	            for _span in span_texts:
490	                shared = _substantive_numerals(_span) & sentence_numbers
491	                for numeral in shared:
492	                    if _marker_binds_numeral_in_span(
493	                        _span, numeral, window, marker_re
494	                    ):
495	                        return False, "binding_qualifier_dropped"
496	
497	    # I-deepfix-001 L4 (#1344): FAIL-CLOSED on unsegmentable script content. A
498	    # claim carrying a run of letters in a script we cannot segment
499	    # (Thai/Lao/Khmer/Myanmar/Tibetan) yields no content tokens; rather than let
500	    # it slip past the overlap floor on a coincidental decimal match, we drop it
501	    # — we cannot establish lexical grounding and MUST NOT guess a pass (the
502	    # lethal weakened-positive). CJK / Arabic / Cyrillic etc. tokenize correctly
503	    # via _content_words and never reach here. Reverts byte-identical under
504	    # PG_STRICT_VERIFY_SCRIPT_AWARE=0.
505	    if has_unsegmentable_content(sentence_clean):
506	        return False, "unsegmentable_script"
507	
508	    # Content-word overlap
509	    sentence_words = _content_words(sentence_clean)
510	    span_words = _content_words(combined_span)
511	    # BUG-03 (FX-02, #1106): a truly contentless sentence — NO content words AND no decimals —
512	    # is dropped EXPLICITLY here (mirrors the provenance_generator floor). With the default
513	    # threshold=2 it would already fail as overlap_too_low, but this fail-closes the
514	    # PG_PROVENANCE_MIN_CONTENT_OVERLAP=0 edge (where overlap=0 is not < 0) and gives a precise
515	    # reason. Faithfulness-TIGHTENING: any content word OR decimal routes to the numeric/overlap
516	    # floors, so a real clinical sentence can never trip this.
517	    if not sentence_words and not sentence_decimals:
518	        return False, "empty_or_contentless_sentence"
519	    overlap = len(sentence_words & span_words)
520	    if overlap < threshold:
521	        return False, "overlap_too_low"
522	
523	    # Check (f) — entailment judge (I-bug-092). Synthesis claims with no
524	    # tokens already short-circuited above; if a synthesis claim DOES
525	    # carry tokens it must clear the same content-correctness bar as
526	    # any other cited sentence.
527	    mode = _entailment_mode()
528	    if mode in ("warn", "enforce"):
529	        verdict, reason = _get_judge().judge(sentence_clean, combined_span)
530	        _record_judge_outcome(verdict, reason)
531	        # I-ready-002 (#1071) P0: the judge FAILS OPEN to ("ENTAILED","judge_error:...") on an API/parse
532	        # error. Counting that as a genuine ENTAILED ships an UNVERIFIED clinical claim as "verified" — a
533	        # silent downgrade of the binding faithfulness gate (LAW II / lethal-in-clinical). Detect it and
534	        # FAIL CLOSED in enforce mode (drop the sentence, like an unsupported one); warn-mode logs only.
535	        if isinstance(reason, str) and reason.startswith("judge_error:"):
536	            # I-deepfix-001 W11-section-verify-judge-error-degrade (#1344): a judge_error is
537	            # a TRANSPORT fault (the judge socket failed / returned unparseable), NOT a
538	            # genuine NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED faithfulness verdict. The mechanical provenance
539	            # gates (a)-(e) ALREADY passed above (evidence-id, span-bounds, decimals, >=2
540	            # content-word overlap), so this is an otherwise span-grounded claim being
541	            # dropped purely because of a socket fault. Under a SUSTAINED judge outage the
542	            # enforce-drop can empty EVERY section -> abort_no_verified_sections despite real
543	            # span-grounded evidence. Per the operator-locked "verifier NEVER holds a report":
544	            # when PG_STRICT_VERIFY_JUDGE_ERROR_ALWAYS_RELEASE is ON, DEGRADE to KEEP-with-
545	            # disclosed-label (the per-claim 'entailment_unverified_judge_error' label IS the
546	            # faithfulness signal). This applies ONLY to judge_error (transport) — genuine
547	            # NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED below stay DROPPED (faithfulness NOT relaxed). Default-OFF
548	            # preserves the byte-identical enforce-drop.
549	            logger.warning(
550	                "entailment judge_error (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r — failing closed",
551	                mode, sentence_clean, reason,
552	            )
553	            if mode == "enforce":
554	                _je_release = os.environ.get(
555	                    "PG_STRICT_VERIFY_JUDGE_ERROR_ALWAYS_RELEASE", "0",
556	                ).strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
557	                if _je_release:
558	                    logger.warning(
559	                        "entailment judge_error (mode=enforce): KEEPING span-grounded "
560	                        "sentence with disclosed label (always-release; transport fault, "
561	                        "not a NEUTRAL/CONTRADICTED verdict): %r", sentence_clean,
562	                    )
563	                    return True, "entailment_unverified_judge_error"
564	                return False, "entailment_judge_error_fail_closed"
565	        if verdict in ("NEUTRAL", "CONTRADICTED"):
566	            logger.warning(
567	                "entailment %s (mode=%s): sentence=%r reason=%r",
568	                verdict, mode, sentence_clean, reason,
569	            )
570	            if mode == "enforce":
571	                return False, "entailment_failed"
572	
573	    return True, None
574

576	def verify_sentence_to_record(
577	    sentence_text: str,
578	    section_id: str,
579	    pool: EvidencePool,
580	    min_content_overlap: int | None = None,
581	    is_synthesis_claim: bool = False,
582	) -> VerifiedSentence:
583	    """Convenience: wrap verify_sentence into a VerifiedSentence record.
584	
585	    Used by the generator orchestrator to build Section.verified_sentences.
586	    """
587	    passed, reason = verify_sentence(
588	        sentence_text,
589	        pool,

596	    # caveat). The VerifiedSentence schema forbids a non-None drop_reason when verifier_pass=True,

604	        section_id=section_id,
605	        sentence_text=sentence_text,

607	        verifier_pass=passed,

609	        kept_disclosure_label=kept_disclosure_label,

1	"""Phase 5: ASSEMBLE — Final report assembly with quality gates.
2	
3	Cross-section dedup, citation resolution, grounded abstract,
4	quality metrics, and the CRITICAL report_assembled trace event
5	that triggers frontend completion.
6	
7	Mostly reuses battle-tested v1 functions. This is the thinnest
8	new code in the v3 pipeline.
9	
10	Failure modes handled:
11	- F5.1: Dedup overcorrection → numeric-aware, max 15% removal
12	- F5.2: Citation resolution → strip invalid, resolve valid to [N]
13	- F5.4: Abstract hallucination → generated from section content only
14	"""
15	
16	import logging
17	import re
18	from typing import Optional
19	
20	from src.polaris_graph.contracts_v3 import (
21	    V3ResultOutput,
22	    VerifiedSectionDraft,
23	)
24	
25	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph")
26	
27	
28	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
29	# Cross-section dedup (F5.1)
30	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
31	
32	def _cross_section_dedup(
33	    sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
34	    max_removal_pct: float = 0.15,
35	) -> list[VerifiedSectionDraft]:
36	    """Remove duplicate sentences across sections.
37	
38	    Numeric-aware: sentences with different numbers are NOT duplicates.
39	    Caps removal at max_removal_pct of total content to prevent over-stripping.
40	    """
41	    if len(sections) < 2:
42	        return sections
43	
44	    # Collect all sentences across all sections with their locations
45	    sentence_locations: dict[str, list[tuple[int, int]]] = {}  # normalized → [(section_idx, sentence_idx)]
46	
47	    section_sentences: list[list[str]] = []
48	    for sec_idx, section in enumerate(sections):
49	        sentences = [s.strip() for s in re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', section.content) if len(s.strip()) > 20]
50	        section_sentences.append(sentences)
51	        for sent_idx, sent in enumerate(sentences):
52	            normalized = sent.lower().strip()
53	            sentence_locations.setdefault(normalized, []).append((sec_idx, sent_idx))
54	
55	    # Find duplicates (appear in 2+ sections)
56	    duplicates_to_remove: set[tuple[int, int]] = set()
57	    total_sentences = sum(len(sents) for sents in section_sentences)
58	    max_removals = max(1, int(total_sentences * max_removal_pct))
59	    removals = 0
60	
61	    for normalized, locations in sentence_locations.items():
62	        if len(locations) < 2:
63	            continue
64	
65	        # Check if sentences contain different numbers (numeric-aware)
66	        original_sentences = [
67	            section_sentences[sec_idx][sent_idx]
68	            for sec_idx, sent_idx in locations
69	            if sent_idx < len(section_sentences[sec_idx])
70	        ]
71	        numbers_per_sentence = [set(re.findall(r'\d+\.?\d*', s)) for s in original_sentences]
72	
73	        # If all instances have the same numbers, it's a true duplicate
74	        # If numbers differ, they're semantically different → keep both
75	        if len(numbers_per_sentence) >= 2:
76	            if numbers_per_sentence[0] != numbers_per_sentence[1]:
77	                continue  # Different numbers → not a duplicate
78	
79	        # Remove from all sections EXCEPT the first occurrence
80	        for sec_idx, sent_idx in locations[1:]:
81	            if removals >= max_removals:
82	                break
83	            duplicates_to_remove.add((sec_idx, sent_idx))
84	            removals += 1
85	
86	    # Rebuild sections without duplicates
87	    result = []
88	    for sec_idx, section in enumerate(sections):
89	        # Re-split from the ORIGINAL content (not just the >20 char sentences)
90	        # to preserve short sentences that weren't in the dedup index
91	        all_sentences = [s.strip() for s in re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', section.content) if s.strip()]
92	        long_sentences = [s.strip() for s in all_sentences if len(s.strip()) > 20]
93	
94	        # Map long sentences to their indices for removal lookup
95	        long_to_remove = set()
96	        for sent_idx, sent in enumerate(section_sentences[sec_idx]):
97	            if (sec_idx, sent_idx) in duplicates_to_remove:
98	                long_to_remove.add(sent.lower().strip())
99	
100	        # Filter original sentences: remove those flagged as duplicates
101	        kept = [s for s in all_sentences if s.lower().strip() not in long_to_remove]
102	        new_content = " ".join(kept) if kept else section.content
103	        result.append(VerifiedSectionDraft(
104	            **{**section.model_dump(), "content": new_content, "word_count": len(new_content.split())}
105	        ))
106	
107	    if removals > 0:
108	        logger.info("[v3 assemble] Cross-section dedup: removed %d duplicate sentences", removals)
109	
110	    return result
111	
112	
113	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
114	# Citation resolution (F5.2)
115	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
116	
117	def _resolve_all_citations(
118	    sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
119	    evidence_store: dict,
120	) -> tuple[list[VerifiedSectionDraft], list[dict]]:
121	    """Resolve all [CITE:ev_xxx] tokens to sequential [N] references.
122	
123	    Returns (resolved_sections, bibliography).
124	    Invalid citations (not in evidence_store) are silently stripped.
125	    """
126	    # Collect all valid cited evidence IDs in order of first appearance
127	    citation_order: list[str] = []
128	    seen: set[str] = set()
129	
130	    for section in sections:
131	        cited = re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', section.content)
132	        for eid in cited:
133	            if eid in evidence_store and eid not in seen:
134	                citation_order.append(eid)
135	                seen.add(eid)
136	
137	    # Build citation map: ev_xxx → sequential number
138	    citation_map: dict[str, int] = {
139	        eid: i + 1 for i, eid in enumerate(citation_order)
140	    }
141	
142	    # Resolve citations in each section
143	    resolved = []
144	    for section in sections:
145	        content = section.content
146	
147	        # Replace valid citations with numbers
148	        def replace_cite(match):
149	            eid = match.group(1)
150	            num = citation_map.get(eid)
151	            if num is not None:
152	                return f"[{num}]"
153	            return ""  # Strip invalid citations
154	
155	        content = re.sub(r'\[CITE:(ev_[a-f0-9]+)\]', replace_cite, content)
156	
157	        # Clean up empty brackets or double spaces from stripped citations
158	        content = re.sub(r'\s{2,}', ' ', content).strip()
159	
160	        resolved.append(VerifiedSectionDraft(
161	            **{**section.model_dump(), "content": content}
162	        ))
163	
164	    # Build bibliography
165	    bibliography = []
166	    for eid in citation_order:
167	        ev = evidence_store.get(eid, {})
168	        num = citation_map[eid]
169	        bibliography.append({
170	            "citation_number": num,
171	            "evidence_id": eid,
172	            "title": ev.get("source_title", "Unknown Source"),
173	            "url": ev.get("source_url", ""),
174	            # M3a (I-deepfix-001): carry DOI/PMID on the v3-assemble bib row too, so a
175	            # contract source reaching the renderer via this path (rather than
176	            # provenance_generator._num_for) keeps a resolvable doi.org/pubmed locator
177	            # instead of dropping to the "no resolvable URL/DOI locator" gap line.
178	            # Additive keys; render-neutral when the locator fallback is OFF.
179	            "doi": ev.get("doi", ""),
180	            "pmid": ev.get("pmid", ""),
181	            "authors": ev.get("authors", []),
182	            "year": ev.get("year", ""),
183	        })
184	
185	    logger.info(
186	        "[v3 assemble] Resolved %d citations → %d bibliography entries",
187	        sum(len(re.findall(r'\[\d+\]', s.content)) for s in resolved),
188	        len(bibliography),
189	    )
190	
191	    return resolved, bibliography
192	
193	
194	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
195	# Grounded abstract (F5.4)
196	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
197	
198	def _generate_abstract(
199	    sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
200	    query: str,
201	    max_words: int = 250,
202	) -> str:
203	    """Generate abstract from actual section content (no LLM needed).
204	
205	    Extracts the first substantive sentence from each section,
206	    prefixed with a scope statement. Pure code — zero hallucination risk.
207	    """
208	    lines = [f"This report examines {query}."]
209	
210	    for section in sections:
211	        if not section.content:
212	            continue
213	        # Extract first substantive sentence (> 30 chars, not a heading)
214	        sentences = [
215	            s.strip() for s in re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', section.content)
216	            if len(s.strip()) > 30 and not s.strip().startswith("#")
217	        ]
218	        if sentences:
219	            lines.append(sentences[0])
220	
221	    abstract = " ".join(lines)
222	
223	    # Truncate to max_words
224	    words = abstract.split()
225	    if len(words) > max_words:
226	        abstract = " ".join(words[:max_words]) + "."
227	
228	    return abstract
229	
230	
231	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
232	# Table of Contents (Fix 7)
233	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
234	
235	def _generate_table_of_contents(
236	    sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
237	) -> str:
238	    """Generate a markdown Table of Contents from resolved sections.
239	
240	    Format: numbered list with section titles.
241	    """
242	    if not sections:
243	        return ""
244	
245	    toc_lines = []
246	    for i, section in enumerate(sections, 1):
247	        toc_lines.append(f"{i}. [{section.title}](#{_slugify(section.title)})")
248	
249	    toc_lines.append(f"{len(sections) + 1}. [References](#references)")
250	
251	    return "\n".join(toc_lines)
252	
253	
254	def _slugify(text: str) -> str:
255	    """Convert section title to URL-safe anchor."""
256	    slug = text.lower().strip()
257	    slug = re.sub(r'[^\w\s-]', '', slug)
258	    slug = re.sub(r'[\s-]+', '-', slug)
259	    return slug
260	
261	
262	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
263	# Quality metrics
264	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
265	
266	def _compute_quality_metrics(
267	    sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
268	    bibliography: list[dict],
269	    report_text: str,
270	) -> dict:
271	    """Compute quality metrics for the final report."""
272	    total_words = len(report_text.split())
273	    total_citations = len(re.findall(r'\[\d+\]', report_text))
274	    unique_sources = len(bibliography)
275	    avg_faithfulness = (
276	        sum(s.faithfulness_score for s in sections) / max(len(sections), 1)
277	    )
278	    sections_with_critic_pass = sum(1 for s in sections if s.critic_passed)
279	
280	    # Analytical depth markers
281	    comparison_markers = len(re.findall(
282	        r'\b(compared to|in contrast|whereas|however|unlike|on the other hand)\b',
283	        report_text, re.I,
284	    ))
285	    table_blocks = len(re.findall(r'\|[^|]+\|[^|]+\|', report_text))
286	
287	    # Chart count (Fix 3 — embedded base64 PNGs)
288	    chart_count = len(re.findall(r'!\[.*?\]\(data:image/png;base64,', report_text))
289	
290	    # NLI verification stats (Fix 4)
291	    nli_verified = sum(s.claims_verified for s in sections)
292	    nli_total = sum(s.claims_total for s in sections)
293	
294	    return {
295	        "word_count": total_words,
296	        "citation_count": total_citations,
297	        "unique_sources": unique_sources,
298	        "citation_density_per_100w": round(total_citations / max(total_words, 1) * 100, 2),
299	        "faithfulness_pct": round(avg_faithfulness * 100, 1),
300	        "sections_total": len(sections),
301	        "sections_critic_passed": sections_with_critic_pass,
302	        "comparison_markers": comparison_markers,
303	        "table_blocks": table_blocks,
304	        "chart_count": chart_count,
305	        "nli_claims_verified": nli_verified,
306	        "nli_claims_total": nli_total,
307	        "has_toc": True,
308	    }
309	
310	
311	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
312	# Main assembler
313	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
314	
315	async def run_assemble_phase(
316	    sections: list[VerifiedSectionDraft],
317	    evidence_store: dict,
318	    query: str,
319	    vector_id: str,
320	    expected_sections: Optional[int] = None,
321	) -> dict:
322	    """Phase 5: Assemble final report from verified sections.
323	
324	    Steps:
325	    1. Cross-section dedup (numeric-aware)
326	    2. Citation resolution ([CITE:ev_xxx] → [N])
327	    3. Build bibliography
328	    4. Generate grounded abstract
329	    5. Compose full report markdown
330	    6. Compute quality metrics
331	    7. Package as V3ResultOutput-compatible dict
332	    """
333	    # Determine status
334	    if expected_sections and len(sections) < expected_sections:
335	        status = "partial"
336	    elif not sections:
337	        status = "failed"
338	    else:
339	        status = "completed"
340	
341	    # Step 1: Cross-section dedup
342	    deduped = _cross_section_dedup(sections)
343	
344	    # Step 2+3: Citation resolution + bibliography
345	    resolved, bibliography = _resolve_all_citations(deduped, evidence_store)
346	
347	    # Step 4: Grounded abstract
348	    abstract = _generate_abstract(resolved, query)
349	
350	    # Step 5: Compose full report with Table of Contents
351	    report_parts = [f"# Research Report: {query}\n"]
352	    if abstract:
353	        report_parts.append(f"## Abstract\n\n{abstract}\n")
354	
355	    # Table of Contents (Fix 7)
356	    toc = _generate_table_of_contents(resolved)
357	    if toc:
358	        report_parts.append(f"## Table of Contents\n\n{toc}\n")
359	
360	    for section in resolved:
361	        report_parts.append(f"## {section.title}\n\n{section.content}\n")
362	
363	    if bibliography:
364	        report_parts.append("## References\n")
365	        for entry in bibliography:
366	            num = entry["citation_number"]
367	            title = entry.get("title", "Unknown")
368	            url = entry.get("url", "")
369	            authors = ", ".join(entry.get("authors", [])) if entry.get("authors") else ""
370	            year = entry.get("year", "")
371	            author_str = f" {authors}." if authors else ""
372	            year_str = f" ({year})" if year else ""
373	            report_parts.append(f"[{num}]{author_str}{year_str} {title}. {url}\n")
374	
375	    final_report = "\n".join(report_parts)
376	
377	    # Step 6: Quality metrics
378	    quality_metrics = _compute_quality_metrics(resolved, bibliography, final_report)
379	
380	    # Step 7: Package as V3ResultOutput-compatible dict
381	    result = {
382	        "vector_id": vector_id,
383	        "original_query": query,
384	        "status": status,
385	        "final_report": final_report,
386	        "bibliography": bibliography,
387	        "quality_metrics": quality_metrics,
388	        "sections": [s.model_dump() for s in resolved],
389	        "evidence": list(evidence_store.values()),
390	        "claims": [],
391	        "iteration_count": 1,
392	        "timestamps": {},
393	        "trace_summary": {},
394	        "v3_metadata": {
395	            "sections_completed": len(resolved),
396	            "sections_expected": expected_sections or len(resolved),
397	            "dedup_applied": len(sections) != len(deduped) or any(
398	                s1.content != s2.content for s1, s2 in zip(sections, deduped)
399	            ),
400	        },
401	    }
402	
403	    logger.info(
404	        "[v3 assemble] Report assembled: %d words, %d citations, %d sources, status=%s",
405	        quality_metrics["word_count"],
406	        quality_metrics["citation_count"],
407	        quality_metrics["unique_sources"],
408	        status,
409	    )
410	
411	    return result
412

def _build_draft(evidence: list) -> str:
    """Construct a draft with correct provenance spans by computing
    offsets from each evidence's direct_quote."""

    def _find_span(ev_id: str, needle: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
        for ev in evidence:
            if ev["evidence_id"] == ev_id:
                idx = ev["direct_quote"].find(needle)
                if idx == -1:
                    raise RuntimeError(
                        f"needle {needle!r} not in ev {ev_id} direct_quote"
                    )
                return idx, idx + len(needle)
        raise RuntimeError(f"evidence_id {ev_id} not found")

    s1 = _find_span("ev_step1", "14.9%")
    s3 = _find_span("ev_step3", "16.0%")
    s5 = _find_span("ev_step5", "17.4%")
    smeta = _find_span("ev_meta", "15.4%")
    snausea = _find_span("ev_nausea", "44.2%")
    sdisc = _find_span("ev_discont", "3.4%")

    # Draft prose. Every numeric claim has a [#ev:id:start-end] token
    # whose span contains the claimed number verbatim.
    draft = (
        f"In the STEP 1 randomized controlled trial, semaglutide 2.4 mg achieved "
        f"a mean weight loss of 14.9% at week 68 [#ev:ev_step1:{s1[0]}-{s1[1]}]. "
        f"The STEP 3 trial combining semaglutide 2.4 mg with intensive behavioral "
        f"therapy produced a mean weight loss of 16.0% at week 68 "
        f"[#ev:ev_step3:{s3[0]}-{s3[1]}]. "
        f"Over 104 weeks of continued therapy, the STEP 5 extension trial "
        f"reported mean weight loss of 17.4% [#ev:ev_step5:{s5[0]}-{s5[1]}]. "
        f"A network meta-analysis ranked semaglutide first with a pooled "
        f"estimate of 15.4% reduction [#ev:ev_meta:{smeta[0]}-{smeta[1]}]. "
        f"On the safety axis, nausea was the most common adverse event, "
        f"occurring in 44.2% of semaglutide recipients [#ev:ev_nausea:{snausea[0]}-{snausea[1]}]. "
        f"Treatment discontinuation due to adverse events was 3.4% in the "
        f"STEP 3 semaglutide arm [#ev:ev_discont:{sdisc[0]}-{sdisc[1]}]."
    )
    return draft

1	"""D8 production release policy — occurrence / residual / S0-must-cover gate.
2	
3	I-meta-002 sub-PR-3. This is the PRODUCTION release gate (a pure function over D8 claim
4	rows; no network, no I/O except the config loader). It is a SEPARATE layer from the
5	FROZEN, pre-registered benchmark scorer (`claim_audit_scorer.py`): this module REUSES the
6	canonical `Verdict` vocabulary (for the type hint only) but does NOT mutate the scorer, does
7	NOT import its private constants, and does NOT change its 0.70 frozen benchmark threshold.
8	
9	The clinical occurrence/residual split (from the I-meta-002 iter-2 D8 design ruling):
10	
11	  * FABRICATED is OCCURRENCE-gated with zero tolerance, via a true one-way LATCH. A
12	    fabricated *citation identity* arrives already stamped `verdict=="FABRICATED"` — the
13	    CALLER (sub-PR-5) runs `judge_contract.classify_unreachable` UPSTREAM with the evidence
14	    pool and writes the result into the row. D8 reads `verdict` only; it never re-classifies
15	    and never receives the evidence pool.
16	  * UNSUPPORTED is RESIDUAL-gated: one rewrite/refuse-in-place attempt, then gate only if the
17	    `CoverageLedger` fraction is below the configured `coverage_threshold`. The ledger
18	    denominator is the FIXED per-question required-element set, so dropping/refusing a claim
19	    LOWERS coverage rather than dodging the gate.
20	  * Genuine UNREACHABLE fetch-misses follow the SAME residual path as UNSUPPORTED (never
21	    silently passed). Only fabricated identities (already `FABRICATED`) occurrence-gate.
22	  * S0 must-cover categories abort release if any required category lacks a VERIFIED claim,
23	    regardless of the overall coverage fraction.
24	  * PARTIAL S0/S1 claims get one rewrite attempt, then ship as a visible advisory gap.
25	
26	Refused-in-place / residual gaps are always emitted as visible `Gap`s — never silent drops.
27	"""

30

32	from dataclasses import dataclass, field

93	    must-cover categories THIS claim addresses (empty if none).

107	@dataclass
108	class CoverageLedger:
109	    """Element-level coverage ledger with a FIXED required-set denominator.
110	
111	    `covered_element_ids` is populated by the CALLER (sub-PR-5) — only required elements
112	    satisfied by a citation-supported VERIFIED claim are added, and genuine UNREACHABLE
113	    elements are excluded by the caller. D8 only reads `.fraction()`. Because the denominator
114	    is the fixed required set, dropping/refusing a claim shrinks the numerator (lowers the
115	    fraction); it can never inflate coverage.
116	    """
117	
118	    required_element_ids: list[str]
119	    covered_element_ids: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
120	
121	    def fraction(self) -> float:
122	        required = set(self.required_element_ids)
123	        if not required:
124	            return 1.0
125	        return len(required & self.covered_element_ids) / len(required)

158	def load_d8_policy_config(path: str | Path | None = None) -> D8PolicyConfig:
159	    """Load the D8 release-policy config from YAML (LAW VI, zero hard-coding).
160	
161	    Fails loudly (FileNotFoundError / KeyError) on a missing file or missing key — never
162	    silently defaults. The per-question required S0 set is passed by the caller, not read
163	    here; `s0_must_cover_categories` is only the default clinical vocabulary.
164	    """
165	    config_path = Path(path) if path is not None else _DEFAULT_CONFIG_PATH
166	    if not config_path.is_file():
167	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"D8 policy config not found: {config_path}")
168	    with config_path.open(encoding="utf-8") as handle:
169	        data = yaml.safe_load(handle)
170	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
171	        raise ValueError(f"D8 policy config is not a mapping: {config_path}")
172	    return D8PolicyConfig(
173	        coverage_threshold=float(data[_CONFIG_KEY_COVERAGE_THRESHOLD]),
174	        material_severities=list(data[_CONFIG_KEY_MATERIAL_SEVERITIES]),
175	        s0_must_cover_categories=list(data[_CONFIG_KEY_S0_MUST_COVER]),
176	    )

179	def apply_d8_release_policy(
180	    d8_rows: list[D8ClaimRow],
181	    *,
182	    required_s0_categories: list[str],
183	    coverage_ledger: CoverageLedger,
184	    coverage_threshold: float,
185	    rewrite_already_attempted: bool,
186	    prior_fabricated_latched: bool = False,
187	) -> ReleaseDecision:
188	    """Apply the D8 production release policy to one pass of claim rows.
189	
190	    Every gate is evaluated (no short-circuit) so `gaps` / `held_reasons` are complete.
191	    `release_allowed` is True iff `held_reasons` is empty; `gaps` and `needs_rewrite` are
192	    separate reporting channels and do NOT by themselves block release.
193	    """
194	    held_reasons: list[str] = []
195	    gaps: list[Gap] = []
196	    needs_rewrite: list[str] = []
197	
198	    material_rows = [row for row in d8_rows if row.is_material]
199	
200	    # (a) FABRICATED occurrence LATCH — one-way; rewrite does NOT clear it.
201	    fabricated_this_pass = any(
202	        row.verdict == _VERDICT_FABRICATED for row in material_rows
203	    )
204	    fabricated_occurrence_latched = prior_fabricated_latched or fabricated_this_pass
205	    if fabricated_occurrence_latched:
206	        held_reasons.append(_REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE)
207	
208	    # (b) UNSUPPORTED residual + (b2) genuine UNREACHABLE residual — SAME path.
209	    # First pass: route material UNSUPPORTED/UNREACHABLE rows to a rewrite/refuse-in-place
210	    # attempt. Post-attempt: emit a VISIBLE residual gap for any that remain (never a silent
211	    # drop). The coverage HOLD below is computed from the ledger alone — NOT from whether a
212	    # residual row is still present — so dropping/refusing the row cannot dodge the gate
213	    # (Codex diff P1-b).
214	    for row in material_rows:
215	        if row.verdict not in (_VERDICT_UNSUPPORTED, _VERDICT_UNREACHABLE):
216	            continue
217	        if not rewrite_already_attempted:
218	            needs_rewrite.append(row.claim_id)
219	        else:
220	            if row.verdict == _VERDICT_UNREACHABLE:
221	                note = (
222	                    "genuine fetch-miss (UNREACHABLE) remained after one rewrite attempt; "
223	                    "visible residual gap, not silently passed"
224	                )
225	            else:
226	                note = "unsupported claim remained after one rewrite/refuse-in-place attempt"
227	            gaps.append(
228	                Gap(
229	                    ref=row.claim_id,
230	                    kind=_GAP_RESIDUAL_UNSUPPORTED,
231	                    severity=row.severity,
232	                    note=note,
233	                )
234	            )
235	    # Coverage floor — UNCONDITIONAL on the fixed-denominator ledger. A run whose required-
236	    # element coverage is below threshold is never releasable, whether the shortfall is caused
237	    # by an UNSUPPORTED/UNREACHABLE row that is still present, one that was dropped/refused, or
238	    # a required element that never had a claim at all. This closes the "drop the row to dodge
239	    # the gate" hole (Codex diff P1-b): the denominator is the required set, so removing a claim
240	    # lowers the fraction. On the first pass a low fraction is additionally caught by the
241	    # pending-rewrite hold below; once the rewrite is exhausted, this is the binding gate.
242	    if coverage_ledger.fraction() < coverage_threshold:
243	        held_reasons.append(_REASON_UNSUPPORTED_RESIDUAL_BELOW_COVERAGE)
244	        gaps.append(
245	            Gap(
246	                ref="__coverage__",
247	                kind=_GAP_COVERAGE_SHORTFALL,
248	                severity="S0",
249	                note=(
250	                    f"required-element coverage {coverage_ledger.fraction():.3f} is below the "
251	                    f"{coverage_threshold:.3f} threshold (fixed required-set denominator)"
252	                ),
253	            )
254	        )
255	
256	    # (c) S0 must-cover gate — category covered iff a VERIFIED claim carries it.
257	    verified_categories: set[str] = set()
258	    for row in d8_rows:
259	        if row.verdict == _VERDICT_VERIFIED:
260	            verified_categories.update(row.s0_categories)
261	    for category in required_s0_categories:
262	        if category not in verified_categories:
263	            held_reasons.append(f"{_REASON_S0_MUST_COVER_MISSING_PREFIX}{category}")
264	            gaps.append(
265	                Gap(
266	                    ref=category,
267	                    kind=_GAP_UNCOVERED_S0,
268	                    severity="S0",
269	                    note=(
270	                        "required S0 must-cover category has no VERIFIED claim "
271	                        "(PARTIAL/citation-only does not satisfy it)"
272	                    ),
273	                )
274	            )
275	
276	    # (d) PARTIAL S0/S1 — one rewrite, then visible advisory gap.
277	    for row in d8_rows:
278	        if row.verdict != _VERDICT_PARTIAL:
279	            continue
280	        if row.severity not in _PARTIAL_REWRITE_SEVERITIES:
281	            continue
282	        if not rewrite_already_attempted:
283	            needs_rewrite.append(row.claim_id)
284	        else:
285	            gaps.append(
286	                Gap(
287	                    ref=row.claim_id,
288	                    kind=_GAP_PARTIAL_ADVISORY,
289	                    severity=row.severity,
290	                    note="PARTIAL claim ships as a visible advisory after one rewrite attempt",
291	                )
292	            )
293	
294	    # A pass with pending rewrites is NOT releasable — the required rewrite/refuse-in-place
295	    # attempt has not happened yet (Codex diff P1-a: release_allowed previously ignored
296	    # needs_rewrite, letting a first-pass run release before the attempt).
297	    #
298	    # I-perm-006 (#1200): `d8_pending_rewrite` is a PHANTOM block — `rewrite_already_attempted` is
299	    # hardcoded False at every call site and NO outer loop ever re-runs the seam to set it True
300	    # (grep: `rewrite_already_attempted=True` exists ONLY in tests/). So it blocks release for an
301	    # attempt the architecture structurally never executes. Under the always-release reframe
302	    # (I-perm-001) an UNSUPPORTED claim ships LABELED via the annotator, not blocked, so the phantom
303	    # block is removed: when PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE is on, `needs_rewrite` stays a pure REPORTING channel
304	    # and does NOT add a held_reason. Flag OFF -> byte-identical (the block still fires).
305	    if needs_rewrite and not always_release_enabled():
306	        held_reasons.append(_REASON_PENDING_REWRITE)
307	
308	    release_allowed = not held_reasons
309	    return ReleaseDecision(
310	        release_allowed=release_allowed,
311	        held_reasons=held_reasons,
312	        gaps=gaps,
313	        needs_rewrite=needs_rewrite,
314	        fabricated_occurrence_latched=fabricated_occurrence_latched,
315	    )

331	# ── I-perm-001 (#1195) keystone slice 1: WITHHOLD → ALWAYS-RELEASE + LABEL ───────────────────
332	# The withhold-when-imperfect gate stack (coverage-below-threshold, S0-must-cover-missing,
333	# pending-rewrite) BLOCKS the whole report. Under always-release it converts every such hold
334	# from a release-BLOCKER into a DISPLAYED disclosed-gap label; the report ships with honest
335	# per-gap disclosure and the user judges. The ONLY hard line that still withholds a normal
336	# report is (a) a FABRICATED occurrence latch, or (b) true zero-grounding (no VERIFIED claim AND
337	# no usable evidence). A clinical safety floor (all required S0 SAFETY categories disclosed as
338	# gaps / zero VERIFIED safety content) does not HARD-block, but it blocks the NORMAL render and
339	# ships an honest "insufficient safety evidence" report instead (operator decision, blueprint R2).
340	#
341	# B5/B7 (2026-06-14): DEFAULT ON. ONLY an EXPLICIT off token ('0'/'false'/'no'/'off') reproduces
342	# the legacy `release_allowed` decision verbatim (byte-identical). Unset / empty / unrecognized ->
343	# ON. The legacy-regression callers pass the off state EXPLICITLY (compute_release_outcome's
344	# `always_release=False` arg, or env '0') — never an empty string.
345	
346	# Master flag.
347	#
348	# B5/B7 (DUAL_AGREED_PLAN, operator-locked 2026-06-14) — "nothing shall hold the report":
349	# the production DEFAULT is now ON (always-release). Holds become DISPLAYED disclosed-gap labels;
350	# every run ships an honest artifact. The OFF switch is retained for the byte-identical legacy
351	# regression: an EXPLICIT off token (``0`` / ``false`` / ``off`` / ``no``) restores the legacy
352	# withhold decision verbatim. UNSET, EMPTY (``""``), and any UNRECOGNIZED value resolve to the
353	# default ON — a default-ON flag must never SILENTLY withhold a report on an unset/stray/empty
354	# value. The legacy-regression callers never pass ``""``; they pass ``"0"`` (env) or
355	# ``always_release=False`` (the explicit arg to ``compute_release_outcome``).
356	_ENV_ALWAYS_RELEASE = "PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE"
357	_ON_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "yes", "on"})
358	# Explicit OFF tokens — the ONLY way to restore the legacy withhold path. Anything else (unset,
359	# unrecognized) resolves to the default-ON always-release behaviour.
360	_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "no", "off"})
361	
362	# Held reasons that are NON-hard: under always-release they become disclosed-gap labels, not
363	# release blockers. A FABRICATED occurrence is the one held reason that is NEVER a mere label.
364	_NON_HARD_HELD_REASONS = frozenset(
365	    {
366	        _REASON_UNSUPPORTED_RESIDUAL_BELOW_COVERAGE,
367	        _REASON_PENDING_REWRITE,
368	    }
369	)

371	# Status vocabulary (mirrors scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES; slice 1 adds
372	# the two released-with-disclosure terminals — both are RELEASED, not abort).
373	STATUS_SUCCESS = "success"
374	STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS = "released_with_disclosed_gaps"
375	STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY = "released_insufficient_safety_evidence"
376	STATUS_ABORT_NO_VERIFIED = "abort_no_verified_sections"
377	STATUS_ABORT_FOUR_ROLE_HELD = "abort_four_role_release_held"
378	STATUS_ABORT_FABRICATED = "abort_evaluator_critical"

380	# B5/B7 (DUAL_AGREED_PLAN §B5/B7, operator-RATIFIED 2026-06-14): a FABRICATED citation is an
381	# INVENTED source not in the evidence pool — it can never be honestly LABELED (there is no real
382	# basket to disclose). The ratified disposition is: DROP that one claim with a LOUD disclosure and
383	# still SHIP the report, rather than HARD-blocking the whole report. The fabricated claim's prose
384	# is removed by the report_redactor (verdict set includes FABRICATED); this gap reason surfaces the
385	# drop as a DISPLAYED label so the drop is never silent. This is NOT a faithfulness relaxation: the
386	# fabricated claim is still detected and still excised from asserted prose — only the REPORT-LEVEL

391	def is_hard_block(
392	    *,
393	    fabricated_occurrence_latched: bool,
394	    zero_verified: bool,
395	    zero_usable_evidence: bool,
396	    redaction_active: bool = True,
397	) -> bool:
398	    """The canonical no-fabrication hard line — narrowed per B5/B7 (operator-ratified 2026-06-14).
399	
400	    A report is HARD-blocked iff there is true zero-grounding (no VERIFIED claim AND no usable
401	    evidence), OR a FABRICATED occurrence latched WHILE per-claim redaction is NOT active.
402	
403	    ``redaction_active`` (default True — production leaves ``PG_REDACT_HELD_UNSUPPORTED`` ON, the
404	    kill-switch is test/offline-only) is the SAFETY COUPLING the narrowing depends on: a fabricated
405	    claim is allowed to ship-minus-itself ONLY because the redactor guarantees the fabricated claim's
406	    prose is excised. If redaction is disabled, the fabricated claim could ship as asserted prose, so
407	    FABRICATED REMAINS a hard block — the narrowing is gated on the excision being guaranteed.
408	    Zero-grounding is ALWAYS a hard block regardless of redaction.
409	    """
410	    if zero_verified and zero_usable_evidence:
411	        return True
412	    if fabricated_occurrence_latched and not redaction_active:
413	        return True
414	    return False

417	@dataclass
418	class ReleaseOutcome:
419	    """The always-release-aware release outcome over a D8 ``ReleaseDecision`` (I-perm-001).
420	
421	    ``released`` — a report ships (normal OR honest-insufficient-safety variant).
422	    ``hard_block`` — the no-fabrication hard line fired; NO report ships as clean.
423	    ``normal_release_blocked`` — the polished normal report is withheld (hard_block OR the clinical
424	        safety floor is insufficient), but an honest report may still ship.
425	    ``status`` — the terminal pipeline status.
426	    ``disclosed_gaps`` — the held reasons surfaced as DISPLAYED labels (not blockers).
427	    ``release_quality_score`` — the displayed coverage fraction (NOT a trap-door threshold).
428	    """
429	
430	    released: bool
431	    hard_block: bool
432	    normal_release_blocked: bool
433	    status: str
434	    disclosed_gaps: list[str]
435	    hard_block_reasons: list[str]
436	    release_quality_score: float
437	    safety_floor: str  # "ok" | "insufficient"
438	    # A18/A2-SEAM STRUCTURAL PROOF FIELDS (iarch007 SWEEP-P0 + RELEASE-P0). These three fields
439	    # let `assert_release_invariant` decide STRUCTURALLY (not by sniffing a gap-label string)
440	    # whether un-judged prose may ship. ALL THREE default to the SAFE (un-proven / fail-closed)
441	    # value so an outcome built without proof can never pass the invariant by omission:
442	    #   * adjudicated — the four-role D8 judge TRULY ran (backed by real final_verdicts). Defaults
443	    #     FALSE: a release that does not explicitly prove adjudication is treated as un-judged.
444	    #   * body_withheld — the findings body was suppressed (the seam fabrication-screen could not
445	    #     run / found a fabricated identity). Defaults FALSE (body ships) — explicit.
446	    #   * compensating_screen_passed — on a seam (no D8) the standalone fabrication screen ran
447	    #     CLEAN, so a body that ships is screen-safe. Defaults FALSE (no compensating proof).
448	    adjudicated: bool = False
449	    body_withheld: bool = False
450	    compensating_screen_passed: bool = False
451	
452	    def display_quality_score(self) -> str:
453	        """B11 C3 (#1362 deepfix): the HONEST display string for the release quality score.
454	
455	        ``release_quality_score`` is the coverage fraction. On a four-role D8 SEAM error (judge
456	        transport / HTTP-400) the judge NEVER adjudicated, so ``adjudicated`` is False and the raw
457	        float is 0.0 (or a coverage fraction that was never judged). Rendering that bare ``0.0`` as
458	        a quality score is a LIE — it reads as "this report scored zero quality" when the truth is
459	        "D8 could not score it at all". This helper renders ``N/A (D8 unadjudicated)`` whenever the
460	        judge did not adjudicate, and the numeric score otherwise. The raw float field is left
461	        unchanged for backward-compatible numeric consumers; the render/UI seam should consult THIS
462	        display string. Faithfulness-neutral: pure presentation of the already-computed state.
463	        """
464	        if not self.adjudicated:
465	            return "N/A (D8 unadjudicated)"
466	        return f"{self.release_quality_score:.3f}"
467

469	def always_release_enabled() -> bool:
470	    """PG_ALWAYS_RELEASE — production DEFAULT ON (B5/B7: "nothing shall hold the report").
471	
472	    OFF only on an EXPLICIT off token ('0'/'false'/'no'/'off'). Unset, EMPTY (''), OR any
473	    unrecognized value resolves to the default (ON) — a default-ON flag must never SILENTLY
474	    withhold a report on an unset/empty/typo value. The OFF switch is the byte-identical
475	    legacy-regression escape hatch; legacy callers pass it explicitly ('0' via env, or
476	    always_release=False to compute_release_outcome) and never as an empty string.
477	    """
478	    import os
479	
480	    return os.environ.get(_ENV_ALWAYS_RELEASE, "").strip().lower() not in _OFF_VALUES

483	def compute_release_outcome(
484	    decision: ReleaseDecision,
485	    *,
486	    zero_verified: bool,
487	    zero_usable_evidence: bool,
488	    safety_floor_insufficient: bool,
489	    coverage_fraction: float,
490	    always_release: bool | None = None,
491	    redaction_active: bool = True,
492	    seam_unadjudicated: bool = False,
493	    fabrication_screen_ran: bool | None = None,
494	    fabrication_screen_found_fabrication: bool = False,
495	) -> ReleaseOutcome:
496	    """Map a D8 ``ReleaseDecision`` to a release outcome under the always-release reframe.
497	
498	    When always-release is OFF, reproduce the LEGACY decision verbatim: ``released`` ==
499	    ``decision.release_allowed``, the held reasons stay blockers, and the status is the legacy
500	    held/success label — byte-identical behaviour. When ON, convert non-hard holds to disclosed
501	    gaps and release, keeping only the zero-grounding hard line and the clinical safety-floor
502	    normal-render block.
503	
504	    ``redaction_active`` (default True — production default; the kill-switch is test/offline-only):
505	    B5/B7 (operator-ratified 2026-06-14) NARROWS the FABRICATED block. With redaction active, a
506	    latched FABRICATED occurrence ships the report MINUS the fabricated claim (excised by the
507	    report_redactor) with a LOUD disclosed-gap label, rather than hard-blocking the whole report.
508	    Only TRUE zero-grounding remains a whole-report hard block. If redaction is disabled, FABRICATED
509	    stays a hard block (the fabricated prose could otherwise ship) — see ``is_hard_block``.
510

648	# The un-judged-release leak this whole block CLOSES: a four-role D8 seam error (the judge could
649	# not be reached) means D8 NEVER adjudicated. The OLD ReleaseOutcome had no way to RECORD that —
650	# the runtime seam builder defaulted to `adjudicated=True`, and the invariant accepted ANY
651	# non-empty disclosed_gaps as "seam proof". Either gap let an un-judged, un-screened body ship.
652	# These three structural fields + the prefix-aware detector + the fail-closed invariant make a
653	# seam release provable ONLY when (a) the body is withheld OR (b) a standalone fabrication screen
654	# ran clean AND the SPECIFIC seam gap is disclosed. Faithfulness: RECORDS the un-judged state and
655	# REFUSES an unsafe release — it relaxes nothing and marks nothing verified.
656	
657	# The SPECIFIC disclosed-gap label the A2 seam rescue injects (matches scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3
658	# SEAM_GAP_UNADJUDICATED + iarch007_release_invariant_check._SEAM_GAP_TOKEN — one vocabulary).
659	_GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED = "four_role_seam_unadjudicated"
660	# The disclosed label surfaced when the standalone seam fabrication screen could NOT run (so the
661	# body is withheld fail-closed — the withhold is never silent).
662	_GAP_SEAM_FABRICATION_SCREEN_UNAVAILABLE = "four_role_seam_fabrication_screen_unavailable"

665	def _has_seam_unadjudicated_gap(disclosed_gaps: list[str]) -> bool:
666	    """True iff ``disclosed_gaps`` carries the SPECIFIC four_role_seam_unadjudicated label.
667	
668	    PREFIX-AWARE (not a blanket substring): the seam disclosure is either the bare constant OR the
669	    descriptive runtime form ``"four_role_seam_unadjudicated: <reason>"`` that
670	    build_seam_release_outcome emits. A gap that merely CONTAINS the token mid-string (e.g.
671	    ``"note: four_role_seam_unadjudicated happened"``) is NOT the seam label — accepting it would
672	    re-open the iarch007 SWEEP-P0 bypass where an arbitrary gap passed as seam proof. So the match
673	    is: equality with the constant, OR a prefix of ``"<constant>:"``.
674	    """
675	    for gap in disclosed_gaps or []:
676	        g = str(gap)
677	        if g == _GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED:
678	            return True
679	        if g.startswith(f"{_GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED}:"):
680	            return True
681	    return False

684	def _compute_seam_outcome(
685	    decision: ReleaseDecision,
686	    *,
687	    zero_verified: bool,
688	    zero_usable_evidence: bool,
689	    safety_floor_insufficient: bool,
690	    coverage_fraction: float,
691	    always_release: bool | None,
692	    redaction_active: bool,
693	    fabrication_screen_ran: bool | None,
694	    fabrication_screen_found_fabrication: bool,
695	) -> ReleaseOutcome:
696	    """The judge-SEAM release outcome (D8 never adjudicated). FAIL-CLOSED.
697	
698	    ``adjudicated`` is ALWAYS False on a seam (the judge did not run). A non-empty seam disclosure
699	    is ALWAYS injected so the outcome can only resolve to released_with_disclosed_gaps, never
700	    success. The body ships ONLY when a standalone fabrication screen PROVES it safe:
701	      * ``fabrication_screen_ran`` is None (UNKNOWN) or False (could not run) -> body WITHHELD, the
702	        unavailable-screen gap disclosed (P0 #3: an un-screened seam body never ships);
703	      * ``fabrication_screen_ran`` True AND found a fabricated identity -> body WITHHELD;
704	      * ``fabrication_screen_ran`` True AND found nothing -> body ships, compensating_screen_passed.
705	
706	    A true zero-grounding (no VERIFIED claim AND no usable evidence) or a fabricated occurrence with
707	    redaction OFF is STILL a whole-report hard block on a seam (the same no-fabrication hard line as
708	    the non-seam path); it is checked first so an un-grounded seam never ships.
709	    """
710	    enabled = always_release_enabled() if always_release is None else always_release
711	    safety_floor = "insufficient" if safety_floor_insufficient else "ok"
712	
713	    # The seam disclosure (bare constant; the runtime descriptive form is added by the caller in
714	    # scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.build_seam_release_outcome — the prefix-aware detector matches both).
715	    disclosed_gaps: list[str] = [_GAP_FOUR_ROLE_SEAM_UNADJUDICATED]
716	
717	    # The no-fabrication hard line still applies on a seam — a zero-grounding or
718	    # fabricated-with-redaction-off body must not ship even un-judged.
719	    hard_block = is_hard_block(
720	        fabricated_occurrence_latched=decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched,
721	        zero_verified=zero_verified,
722	        zero_usable_evidence=zero_usable_evidence,
723	        redaction_active=redaction_active,
724	    )
725	    if (not enabled) or hard_block:
726	        hard_block_reasons: list[str] = []
727	        if zero_verified and zero_usable_evidence:
728	            hard_block_reasons.append("zero_grounding")
729	        if decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched and not redaction_active:
730	            hard_block_reasons.append(_REASON_FABRICATED_OCCURRENCE)
731	        status = (
732	            STATUS_ABORT_FABRICATED
733	            if (decision.fabricated_occurrence_latched and not redaction_active)
734	            else STATUS_ABORT_FOUR_ROLE_HELD
735	            if not enabled
736	            else STATUS_ABORT_NO_VERIFIED
737	        )
738	        # Fail-closed seam hold: body withheld, never adjudicated.
739	        return ReleaseOutcome(
740	            released=False,
741	            hard_block=hard_block,
742	            normal_release_blocked=True,
743	            status=status,
744	            disclosed_gaps=disclosed_gaps,
745	            hard_block_reasons=hard_block_reasons,
746	            release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
747	            safety_floor=safety_floor,
748	            adjudicated=False,
749	            body_withheld=True,
750	            compensating_screen_passed=False,
751	        )
752	
753	    # always-release ON, not hard-blocked: decide the body disposition from the screen result.
754	    # ALWAYS-RELEASE PRINCIPLE (operator-locked: the verifier is a LABEL, never a HOLD): the D8 safety
755	    # floor being unconfirmed does NOT withhold the body — the report ALWAYS ships and the unadjudicated
756	    # state is DISCLOSED so the user judges (status=released_insufficient_safety_evidence + the seam
757	    # disclosure below). safety_floor_insufficient is a LABEL, not a gate. (An earlier change wrongly
758	    # forced a withhold here; reverted per operator 2026-06-20 — see feedback_always_release.)
759	    screen_clean = fabrication_screen_ran is True and not fabrication_screen_found_fabrication
760	    body_withheld = not screen_clean
761	    compensating_screen_passed = screen_clean
762	    if not screen_clean:
763	        # The body is withheld — surface WHY so the withhold is never silent (P0 #3).
764	        disclosed_gaps.append(_GAP_SEAM_FABRICATION_SCREEN_UNAVAILABLE)
765	
766	    status = (
767	        STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY
768	        if safety_floor_insufficient
769	        else STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS
770	    )
771	    return ReleaseOutcome(
772	        released=True,
773	        # The body is suppressed when withheld OR when the clinical safety floor blocks the normal
774	        # render; either way the polished normal report is not shipped as clean.
775	        hard_block=False,
776	        normal_release_blocked=body_withheld or safety_floor_insufficient,
777	        status=status,
778	        disclosed_gaps=disclosed_gaps,
779	        hard_block_reasons=[],
780	        release_quality_score=coverage_fraction,
781	        safety_floor=safety_floor,
782	        adjudicated=False,
783	        body_withheld=body_withheld,
784	        compensating_screen_passed=compensating_screen_passed,

788	class ReleaseInvariantError(RuntimeError):
789	    """Raised by ``assert_release_invariant`` when a release-asserting outcome cannot PROVE the
790	    four-role D8 judge adjudicated, OR (on a seam) that the un-judged body is provably safe.
791	
792	    This is the in-process, structural twin of scripts/iarch007_release_invariant_check (the
793	    artifact-layer CI gate). It is the LAST fail-closed line before the success-path manifest
794	    write: a path that mis-builds the A2 seam rescue (auto-releasing un-judged content as a
795	    release-asserting status) raises here rather than shipping an un-judged report.
796	    """
797	
798	
799	# Release-asserting statuses: a report ships as clean/judge-final OR honest-disclosed. Each demands
800	# proof of adjudication OR a provably-safe seam disposition. abort_*/error_*/partial_* are NOT
801	# release-asserting (the invariant is a no-op on them — no false trip).
802	_RELEASE_ASSERTING_STATUSES = frozenset(
803	    {
804	        STATUS_SUCCESS,
805	        STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS,
806	        STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY,
807	    }
808	)

811	def assert_release_invariant(outcome: ReleaseOutcome) -> ReleaseOutcome:
812	    """Fail-closed: refuse any release-asserting outcome that cannot PROVE it is judge-safe.
813	
814	    Returns ``outcome`` unchanged on PASS; raises ``ReleaseInvariantError`` on a violation. The
815	    proof is read STRUCTURALLY from the outcome fields (status + adjudicated + body_withheld +
816	    compensating_screen_passed + the prefix-aware seam-gap detector), NEVER by sniffing an
817	    arbitrary gap-label string. The accepted shapes (and ONLY these):
818	
819	      (a) the four-role D8 judge TRULY adjudicated — ``adjudicated=True`` — AND this is NOT a seam
820	          (a seam disclosure with adjudicated=True is a CONTRADICTION: the default-True-on-seam leak
821	          this closes), OR
822	      (b) the SPECIFIC ``four_role_seam_unadjudicated`` gap is disclosed AND a compensating
823	          standalone fabrication screen ran clean (``compensating_screen_passed`` — leg-3 is COUPLED
824	          to the specific seam gap; a bare screen flag without the disclosure is NOT proof), OR
825	      (c) the findings body is WITHHELD (``body_withheld`` — nothing un-judged ships).
826	
827	    It does NOT accept ``adjudicated=True`` by itself when a seam gap is present (that is the
828	    contradiction), and does NOT accept an arbitrary non-empty disclosed_gaps list as seam proof.
829	    Non-release-asserting statuses (abort_*/error_*/partial_*) pass untouched. A ``released``
830	    that contradicts an abort status is also a violation.
831	    """
832	    status = str(outcome.status or "")
833	    seam_gap = _has_seam_unadjudicated_gap(outcome.disclosed_gaps)
834	
835	    # Contradiction guard (fires for ANY status, before the per-status legs): a seam disclosure
836	    # means the judge did NOT adjudicate, so adjudicated=True alongside the seam gap is the
837	    # default-True-on-seam leak — fail closed.
838	    if seam_gap and outcome.adjudicated:
839	        raise ReleaseInvariantError(
840	            "release outcome carries the four_role_seam_unadjudicated gap AND adjudicated=True — a "
841	            "contradiction (a judge seam-error means D8 never adjudicated). This is the "
842	            "default-True-on-seam leak; the seam outcome MUST carry adjudicated=False."
843	        )
844	
845	    if status not in _RELEASE_ASSERTING_STATUSES:
846	        # Not release-asserting. The only remaining check is a released/abort contradiction:
847	        # a `released`-True outcome whose status is an abort is a self-contradiction.
848	        if outcome.released and status.startswith("abort"):
849	            raise ReleaseInvariantError(
850	                f"released=True but status={status!r} (an abort) — the binding release decision "
851	                "contradicts the terminal status."
852	            )
853	        return outcome
854	
855	    # The seam-rescue proof (the ONLY way a release-asserting status may ship un-judged): the
856	    # SPECIFIC seam gap is disclosed AND the body is provably safe — withheld OR a compensating
857	    # fabrication screen ran clean. An arbitrary disclosed gap is NOT proof (SWEEP-P0).
858	    seam_rescue_proven = seam_gap and (
859	        outcome.body_withheld or outcome.compensating_screen_passed
860	    )
861	
862	    if outcome.adjudicated or seam_rescue_proven:
863	        return outcome
864	
865	    # No real D8 adjudication and no proven-safe seam disposition. A body withheld (even without
866	    # the seam gap) is still a terminal SAFE disposition — nothing un-judged ships — so accept it.
867	    if outcome.body_withheld:
868	        return outcome
869	
870	    raise ReleaseInvariantError(
871	        f"status={status!r} asserts a release, but there is NO D8 adjudication "
872	        "(adjudicated=False) and NO PROVEN seam rescue (the specific four_role_seam_unadjudicated "
873	        "label PLUS a withheld body OR a passed compensating fabrication screen) and the body is NOT "
874	        "withheld. A released report with no real judging and no proven-safe disposition is a silent "
875	        "un-judged release — refused fail-closed."
876	    )

880	# The manifest ``release_disclosure.release_quality_score`` was set VERBATIM to the coverage
881	# fraction. On the autopsy'd run that fraction was 1.0 while the evaluator rule-gate had returned
882	# ``gate_class=abort`` (a PT11 uncited-numeric failure) — so the scorecard read GREEN (1.000)
883	# while a real, disclosed deficiency existed. A required-entity slot that was left empty was
884	# masked the same way. This block re-derives the DISPLAYED quality score so a genuine deficiency
885	# pulls the scorecard BELOW green.
886	#
887	# FAITHFULNESS: presentation only. This changes NO gate, NO per-claim verdict, and NO verified
888	# flag. strict_verify / NLI entailment / the four-role D8 judge / provenance / span-grounding are
889	# all untouched — the score can only be LOWERED (never raised), and no un-adjudicated release is
890	# ever made green (an unadjudicated outcome keeps its "N/A (D8 unadjudicated)" display).
891	
892	# The lowest release-quality score the scorecard still renders GREEN. A run with a disclosed
893	# deficiency must land strictly below this. Env-overridable per LAW VI (zero hard-coding).
894	_ENV_GREEN_QUALITY_FLOOR = "PG_RELEASE_QUALITY_GREEN_FLOOR"
895	_DEFAULT_GREEN_QUALITY_FLOOR = 0.85
896	# The ceiling a run's quality score is capped at when the evaluator rule-gate returned an abort
897	# class (the strongest non-fatal deficiency signal). Well below the green floor.
898	_ENV_ABORT_QUALITY_CEILING = "PG_RELEASE_QUALITY_ABORT_CEILING"
899	_DEFAULT_ABORT_QUALITY_CEILING = 0.5
